name: discord-qdrant-hybrid-search-combining-max-score-fusion
prompt: Is there a way to apply a maximum expression over the raw scores of multiple prefetches? Let's say we have a collection with three named vectors (title, abstract, and body), all generated from the same embedding model. We want to run a query against all three named vectors and merge the candidates by taking the maximum raw score across the named vectors for each document. Why not RRF? We care about the absolute score rather than just position/rank. Why not DBSF? We don't want the scores to be normalized across the prefetched distributions. Example: Doc 1: title 0.85, abstract 0.82, body 0.80 => Max Score 0.85. Doc 2: title 0.40, abstract 0.50, body 0.92 => Max Score 0.92. Since Doc 2's body (0.92) is a better match than anything in Doc 1, our final results should rank Doc 2 above Doc 1. We looked into using FormulaQuery to access $score, but couldn't find a way to express a MaxExpression (only SumExpression, MultExpression, etc. seem natively supported). Does Qdrant have a built-in way to handle Maximum expressions directly in a FormulaQuery or via another method in the Query API? If not, what would be the recommended workaround?

rubric.text: States that Qdrant does not currently have a built-in MaxExpression inside FormulaQuery
Offers the smooth-max (log-sum-exp) approximation as a server-side workaround using FormulaQuery's exp, ln, sum, mult operations, in the form max ≈ (1/k) * ln(sum(exp(k * score_i)))
Notes this smooth-max approach is an approximation, not a true max, and works better when raw scores are in a stable range (e.g. cosine similarity) but may be sensitive with larger dot-product scores
Offers client-side max as the workaround for an exact result: query each named vector, group results by point ID, take max(score), sort by max score
Concludes there is no native true max across named vectors, and that client-side max is the recommended option if an exact result is required
Mentions RecommendBestScore as an existing max-style behavior, but clarifies it applies within a single named vector's recommend query and does not solve max across multiple named vectors
Claims or fabricates that a MaxExpression (or equivalent native max operation) exists in FormulaQuery
